children.text: Commissioners; appointment, number, and compensation; term of office; vacancy

The United States shall be represented on the Commission by 4 Commissioners who shall be appointed by the President and who may not receive compensation for service as Commissioners. Of the Commissioners—
1 shall be an official of the United States Government; and
3 shall be individuals who reside in different Great Lakes States and who are knowledgeable regarding the fisheries of the Great Lakes, except that 1 of them must also be an official of

children.tail: So in original. Probably should be followed by “a”.

children.text: The President shall appoint an alternate Commissioner who shall perform the duties of a Commissioner—
until a vacancy referred to in subsection (b)(3) is filled; and
in the event of the absence of a Commissioner from any meeting of the United States Section or the Commission.

Individuals serving as such Commissioners shall not be considered to be Federal employees while performing such service, except for purposes of injury compensation or tort claims liability as provided in chapter 81 of title 5 and chapter 171 of title 28.
Except as provided in paragraph (2), the term of office of Commissioners appointed under subsection (a)(1)(B) is 6 years.
Of the Commissioners first appointed under subsection (a)(1)(B) after

children.tail: , 1 shall be appointed for a term of 2 years, 1 shall be appointed for a term of 4 years, and 1 shall be appointed for a term of 6 years.

children.text: Whenever a vacancy occurs among Commissioners appointed under subsection (a)(1)(B), the President shall appoint an individual to fill that vacancy for the remainder of the applicable term.

children.tail: amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “The United States shall be represented on the Commission by three Commissioners to be appointed by the President, to serve as such during his pleasure, and to receive no compensation for their services as such Commissioners. Of such Commissioners—

children.text: “(a) one shall be an official of the United States Government; and
“(b) two shall be persons residing in Great Lakes States, duly qualified by reason of knowledge of the fisheries of the Great Lakes, of whom one shall be an official of a Great Lakes State:

children.tail: , That the Commissioners appointed under this subsection shall not be residents of the same State.”

children.text: Termination and Transitional Service of Incumbent Commissioners
“The term of office of each United States Commissioner on the Great Lakes Fishery Commission who is serving on the date of enactment of this Act [

children.tail: ] is terminated (except the United States Government official appointed under section 3(a) of the Great Lakes Fishery Act of 1956 (

, as in effect before the date of enactment). However, the individuals appointed to those terms shall continue to serve as Commissioners until the President makes appointments under section 3(b)(2) of the Act of 1956 (as added by subsection (a)), which appointments shall be made within 60 days after the date of enactment.”

children.text: Secretary of State authorized to designate Alternate United States Commissioners, see sections 2672a and 2672b of Title 22, Foreign Relations and Intercourse.
